title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for bounded worker pool, origin fairness, cancellation, and shutdown and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
Assign producers, closers, receivers, buffers, and terminal states so sends, closes, and drains cannot race or block forever.
Prevent one publisher from consuming all slots while preserving useful throughput and explicit per-origin limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Concurrency Budget · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Concurrency Budget
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 1118 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove bounded origin-aware worker ownership, failure isolation, cancellation, and joined shutdown; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop claims, cancel fetches, bound drain time, join workers, finalize or abandon leases safely, close pools, and report incomplete work.
